bridge1_onboarding_image_alt: Juan Diego Saire — UX Engineer and DesignOps Specialist.
bridge1_onboarding_heading: I take products past the workshops and journey maps and into production.
bridge1_onboarding_heading_2: A strategist who reads the code, runs the repo, and directs the build.
attribution: Juan Diego Saire — UX Engineer and DesignOps Specialist.
work_lede: Three builds where the call and the code were mine.
work_card1_heading: A design system I built and govern end to end.
work_card1_body: My own site is the operating layer I run: global tokens, 10 reusable organisms, and a 175-key bilingual engine, hand-written in HTML/CSS/JS at WCAG AA. Every component is held to correct adoption — proven on the page you're reading.
work_card2_heading: The TUUA platform I designed, prototyped, and handed to engineering.
work_card2_body: At Lima Airport Partners I designed, prototyped, and evaluated the TUUA international-transfer fee platform, then delivered the user flow and mockups to engineering for build. It's live, serving 130,000+ international-transfer passengers monthly.
work_card3_heading: A high-fidelity prototype of the full airport passenger journey.
work_card3_body: Before I applied to Lima Airport, I built LimaFly: a high-fidelity prototype of the complete passenger journey, on a scalable foundation of tokens, component variants, and Auto Layout. I prototype the industry to walk in already fluent.
capabilities_lede: Not a commodity programmer — the strategist who calls feasibility, then builds.
cap_s1_body: I make the feasibility and viability call up front, design the UX, and take it to production — so a strategic decision becomes a shipped product, not a deck.
cap_s2_body: I define the tokens, components, and protocols once, then keep adoption correct and consistent across the whole portfolio — turning a shared standard into something local teams can actually run, day to day.
cap_s3_body: I build and automate to multiply what internal teams can do — sharing AI assistants and know-how that lift productivity and the quality of deliverables across the workforce. The capability multiplier that turns curiosity into learning, learning into solutions, and solutions into real impact.
evolution_lede: I came down from strategy and picked up the build. Here's the arc.
evo_m1_body: Industrial & Commercial Engineering, specialization Corporate Finance. The systems-and-process grounding under everything since.
evo_m2_body: South America's first LNG plant. Held contractor deliverables to quality, schedule, and spec across 35 EPC projects — vendor governance, learned early.
evo_m3_body: Full-time, three continents (UVic, Canada; Maastricht, Netherlands). First in class. Strategic Consulting.
evo_m4_body: Taught 1,200+ students; mentored 29 project teams through end-to-end UX; seeded Communities of Practice.
evo_m5_body: MicroMasters in UX Design: the full UX lifecycle, including a UX-management track on team operations and process standardization.
evo_m5_note: Google UX Design (2025) · Google GenAI Leader (2025) · Google Project Management (2025).
evo_m6_body: Innovation's seat on the most critical post-terminal program: designed and delivered the TUUA payment platform (now 130,000+ passengers monthly), articulated planning across five central areas — Finance, IT, Terminals, Commercial, Reputation — and trained 70 staff to 3.88/4.
evo_m6_note: McKinsey Forward — McKinsey.org (2025) · Microsoft Program Management (2026).
evo_m7_body: Designed, built, and govern a full system end to end: tokens, 10 organisms, a 175-key bilingual engine, hand-written, WCAG AA.
evo_m8_body: Bringing this systemic way of working to a design organization scaling one system across many markets.
tr_stat5_label: Keys in the bilingual engine I built and run
ticker_logo_utec_alt: UTEC University of Engineering and Technology
contact_lede: Tell me about the team and the challenge.
contact_msg_placeholder: Describe the scope, the expected role, and the timeline. The precision of these details will determine whether the engagement is viable.
contact_disclaimer: I'll reply within 48 hours — usually sooner.
